summary: Completion Reviewer 03 inspected dinobrain and dinobrain-data from the retrieval/LLM Wiki lens. Conclusion: AGREE with the index-first, graph-aware, provenance-aware hybrid retrieval direction, but DISAGREE that the current LLM Wiki is complete. Evidence: live Context Packs report lexical_fallback_v2, the data repo has wiki-index.json but no wiki.sqlite shard, current context eval fails on noise despite full recall, behavior eval is blocked by missing behavior-golden.json, and graph-health appears stale relative to the current wiki index.
decisions: LLM Wiki completion must require fast indexed candidate selection, honest retrieval-mode reporting, and true dense retrieval only when a usable dense vector index/provider exists.

Operational traces/accepted behavior rules currently create ranking pressure; completion requires durable wiki/source/project knowledge to outrank broad operational behavior rules when the query intent calls for it.
Graph completion is not the visual graph alone; it requires fresh indexed nodes/edges, wikilinks, provenance/lifecycle edges, graph-health freshness, and degraded status when empty or stale.

Quality completion is not satisfied until representative context and behavior golden sets pass under growth/noise budgets, including semantic paraphrase, rare exact queries, provenance, quarantine, and recency cases.
Latency completion should require the prompt path to avoid full vault scans and use SQLite wiki plus operations shards with measured query and rebuild budgets as data grows.
next_steps: Create or refresh the wiki SQLite shard and manifest for the live data repo, then verify runtime candidate_source is sqlite_shards_v2 for wiki_search and get_context_pack.
Replace the bootstrap context-golden cases with representative LLM Wiki cases and add behavior-golden.json for memory lift evaluation.
Tune ranking and/or type-specific budgets so broad accepted behavior rules do not swamp durable wiki/source/project records.
Make graph-health freshness part of the completion gate by comparing it to the current wiki index/shard generation time.
Add scale/latency gates that fail when candidate counts, query latency, or index freshness exceed the agreed budgets.
search_queries: DinoBrain LLM Wiki retrieval completion conditions
hybrid retrieval graph provenance latency eval DinoBrain
